creator: Cy.Schubert@komquats.com
text:
Cheers,
 Cy Schubert <Cy.Schubert@komquats.com>
 FreeBSD UNIX:  <cy@FreeBSD.org>   Web:  http://www.FreeBSD.org
 
 Subject: Re: Partial Solution: ports/173619: security/krb5 revision 306935 
  on Sat Nov 3 broke build on FreeBSD i386 9.1RC3
 In-Reply-To: Message from Robert Burmeister <Robert.Burmeister@UToledo.edu>
    of "Tue, 13 Nov 2012 23:35:40 EST." <50A31F9C.20506@UToledo.edu>
 Mime-Version: 1.0
 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii
 --------
 In message <50A31F9C.20506@UToledo.edu>, Robert Burmeister writes:
 > Very Odd; the jist is /security/krb5 cannot be built if it is already install
 > ed..??
 > The "verto*" files were installed by krb5.
 > I deleted all "verto" files from the system, and was able to build /security/
 > krb5,
 > but was unable to build it after I installed it.
 > 
 > The pkg_which results before deleting the "verto" files were:
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_info | grep "verto"
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which verto.h
 > verto.h: not found
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which verto-module.h
 > verto-module.h: not found
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which libverto.so
 > krb5-1.10.3_1
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which libverto.so.0
 > krb5-1.10.3_1
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which libverto-k5ev.so
 > krb5-1.10.3_1
 > /usr/local/lib% pkg_which libverto-k5ev.so.0
 > krb5-1.10.3_1
 > /usr/local/lib%
 
 I always keep mine in /usr/local/krb5. It was something I did at a previous 
 place of employment when I initially installed it on a bunch of Sun, Tru64, 
 DG, HP, FreeBSD, and Linux systems. My mentor when I was drafted as a 
 FreeBSD committer 11 year ago also did the same. I think that was a 
 standard thing to do at the time. That's why we have a $KRB5_HOME make.conf 
 variable. I'll try building it without KRB5_HOME specified on one of my 
 testbeds to see how it builds. Having said that, I recall building it in a 
 jail (my testbed jail on my laptop) having forgotten to set KRB5_HOME in 
 make.conf. I believe it built while still installed. I'll have to test that 
 again.
 
 I just rebuilt the port without KRB5_HOME set on my testbed (9.1RC 
 partition). It built. I don't know what you did to cause it to fail like 
 this but on the surface I would say that the issue is local to your system.
